description: The scene opens on a sunny park with people sitting on the grass, surrounded by trees and a city skyline in the background.
The setting changes to a living room. Phoebe walks through the front door, wearing an orange jacket over a green shirt. A television plays an indistinct audio track.
Phoebe smiles, looking toward the living area as she asks, "Hey, what you guys doing?"

Richard sits on a sofa next to Monica, holding a crumpled tissue near his eye. "Monica's making us watch Old Yeller," he says. Ross and Rachel sit on another chair; Rachel rests her chin on her hand, looking toward the television.
Phoebe takes off her orange jacket, smiling, and looks at the others.
"Why are you guys so upset? It's Old Yeller. It's a happy movie," Phoebe says, finishing removing her jacket.
Ross turns his head to look at Rachel, drawing his eyebrows together.
Ross looks back toward Phoebe and asks, "What are you talking about?"
"Come on. Happy family gets a dog," Phoebe replies, walking toward the kitchen area and tossing her jacket aside.
"Frontier fun," Phoebe says, standing near a kitchen island and picking up a small cracker.
Phoebe takes a bite of the cracker. Ross turns to look at Rachel again.
Ross looks at Phoebe and says, "Yeah, but Phoebe, what about the..." Phoebe interrupts, her mouth full, "oh, what? When Yeller saves the family from the wolf and everyone's happy?"
Ross replies, "That's not the end."
Phoebe's eyebrows draw together.
"Yah. That's when my mother would shut off the TV and say, the end," Phoebe says, gesturing with her free hand.
Monica turns to look back at Phoebe.
Monica asks, "What about the part where he has rabies?" Phoebe's eyes widen slightly as she gestures with both hands and replies, "He doesn't have rabies. He has babies."
Ross and Rachel look at each other. Rachel rests her hand on Ross's shoulder.
"That's what my mom said," Phoebe adds, nodding slightly.
Richard turns his head to look at Phoebe.
Richard looks over the back of the sofa and says, "Phoebe, I don't think your mom would want you to see what's about to happen."
Phoebe steps forward, looking toward the television, and asks, "Why? What's about to happen? I've"
"never seen this part before," Phoebe finishes, staring straight ahead at the television.
"Hey, Travis, what you doing with that?" Phoebe asks, maintaining her gaze forward.
Ross covers his mouth with his hand, looking at the television. Rachel covers her eyes with a tissue.
"No. No," Phoebe says, dropping her hands to her sides, her eyes wide.
"Travis. Put down the" Phoebe says, pointing toward the television.
Phoebe steps forward, her eyes remaining fixed on the television, her hand still raised.
"he's. He's your buddy. He's your" Phoebe says, her volume rising.
Phoebe raises her hands near her face. Ross presses his face into Rachel's neck, shutting his eyes. A gunshot sounds from the television. Phoebe gasps loudly, covering her ears.
"okay, what kind of a sick doggy snuff film is this?" Phoebe says, her voice trembling slightly as she gestures with both hands in front of her.
The television audio stops. The screen fades entirely to black.
The setting changes to an exterior view of an apartment window at night. A man is visible inside, holding a small item.

Inside the apartment, Monica walks past the counter holding two plates of food. She picks up a cordless phone, holds it to her ear, and says, "Hey, have you guys eaten? Because Richard and I just finished and we've got leftovers"
Monica stops walking.
"chicken and potatoes," Monica continues into the phone.
Monica stands still, listening.
"What am I wearing?" Monica asks into the phone, raising her eyebrows.
Monica looks down briefly.
"Actually, nothing but rubber gloves," Monica says into the phone, gesturing slightly with her free hand.
The door opens, and Chandler rushes in with a cordless phone to his ear, followed closely by Joey. Chandler lowers the phone as he looks at Monica, who remains fully clothed.
Joey places a hand on his hip, gestures toward Monica, and says, "You know, one of these times you're going to really be naked and we're not going to come over."
Monica smiles.
"OOH, that teach me a lesson," Monica replies, her pitch rising.
Chandler looks at Joey, then both walk further into the kitchen area.
Monica looks at Chandler and says, "All right, I've got a leg, three breasts, and a wing." Chandler replies, "Well, how do you find clothes that fit?"
Monica looks at Chandler, her facial expression remaining neutral.
Joey and Chandler sit at the kitchen table. "Oh, hey, Monica. We got a question," Joey says.
Monica walks toward the table, interrupting, "All right, for the millionth time, yes, I see other women in the shower at the gym. And no, I don't look."
"Oh, not that one," Joey says, looking up at Monica.

Joey gestures with his hand and says, "We're trying to figure out who to bring to the Knicks game tonight. We have an extra ticket." Chandler points at himself and Joey, adding, "Yeah, Ross can't go. So it's between"
"my friend Eric Prower, who has breath issues and Dan, with the poking," Chandler says, mimicking a poking motion with his finger.
"Did you see that play? Do" Chandler asks, continuing his gestures.
"you want some more beer?" Chandler continues, mimicking someone turning away and asking a question.
"Is that Spike Lee?" Chandler adds, pointing off to the side while maintaining his mimicry.
"Okay," Monica says, turning slightly.
Richard walks out from a back hallway, adjusting his belt.
"why don't you ask Richard?" Monica says, gesturing toward Richard as she looks at Chandler and Joey.
Chandler turns to look at Richard.
"Okay," Joey says, nodding.
"Hey, Richard, if you had an extra ticket to the Knicks game" Joey says, raising one hand.
Monica looks between Joey and Richard.
"and you had to choose between a friend who smelled and one who bruises you, who would you pick?" Joey asks, gesturing with his hands. Richard looks at Joey.

Richard, standing behind Monica with his hands near his pockets, looks at Joey and Chandler. "Wow. Well, being a huge Knicks fan myself I think you should take someone who's a huge Knicks fan," he says.
Chandler and Joey look at each other.
"Okay," Chandler says softly.
"That's Eric," Chandler adds, nodding at Joey.
Inside an apartment, Chandler and Joey sit at a round dining table. Monica walks away from the table as Richard exits in the background. Monica says, "Glad to be of help." Joey says, "Matches."
Monica walks back toward the table, coming to a stop behind Chandler and Joey.
Monica rests her hands on the backs of their chairs and says, "I meant, why don't you take Richard to the game," followed by overlapping voices responding, "Landon? What?"
Joey turns his head slightly.
Joey says, "I don't know." Monica says, "Come on." Joey adds, "He keeps his fingers to himself, and he's always fresh."
Chandler looks toward Joey and scratches his ear.
Chandler says, "I don't know. Richard's really nice and everything. It's just that we don't know him really know."
Chandler gestures with one hand, adding, "And plus know old"
Chandler pauses, his gaze shifting as he searches for words.
Chandler continues, "er than some people,"
Chandler pauses again, lowering his hand.
Chandler gestures broadly, finishing, "but younger than some buildings."
Monica raises her hands briefly.
Monica gestures with open hands, saying, "So he's a little older. Big deal. I mean, he's important to me."
Monica adds, "If you ask him, he might take you in his Jag."
Joey leans forward across the table toward Chandler, bringing his face closer.
Speaking softly, Joey asks Chandler, "How do we say yes now and make it seem like we're not doing it just to ride in a cool car?"
Chandler rubs his mouth thoughtfully, looking at Joey.
Chandler whispers back, "Okay, this could be"
Chandler turns his head toward Monica and straightens his posture.
Speaking at a normal volume, Chandler says, "cute. Monica, we'll bring him, but only if he takes the Jaguar."
Monica looks at Chandler with a flat expression.
Joey points his finger at Chandler, saying, "OOH, you almost had"
The setting changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night. A yellow taxi drives past the entrance.
Inside an apartment, Ross holds a toddler in his arms, bouncing the child gently.
Ross says to the toddler, "no, no, you're fine. You're fine," as the front door opens behind him.
Carol and Susan walk through the doorway.
Carol greets him, saying, "Hi."
Susan adds a greeting as they step further into the apartment.
Ross turns toward them, saying, "Oh, you guys are not going to believe what happened." Carol asks, "What?"
Ross gestures toward a playpen with his free hand. "Okay, we were sitting over there, playing on the floor and he grabs the table and he pulls himself up."
The toddler is now held by Carol. Ross looks at her excitedly.
Holding the toddler, Carol says, "He pulled himself up?"
Ross nods rapidly.
Ross says, "Standing?"
Ross adds, "Man."
Ross spreads his arms slightly.
Ross says, "I'm sorry you guys missed it."
Ross points toward a nearby camera. "But I did tape it. So if you guys want to see it"
Carol interjects, "we know. He already did it last week. You can watch our tape if you"
Carol finishes, "want." Ross reacts with shock, his arms dropping. "I can't believe this. I missed the first time of everything I missed"
Carol asks, "what?" Ross asks, "The first time he rolled over?"
Ross shakes his head in dismay.
Ross counts on his fingers, asking, "The first time he crawled. What else did I miss? Has he spoken yet?"
Ross raises his eyebrows, waiting for an answer.
Ross extends his hands, asking, "Is he driving? Does he have a favorite liqueur?"
Susan smiles faintly at Ross's questions.
Carol says, "Actually, he's getting closer on the talking thing. He can't quite say Mama yet, but once he said Yemen."
Ross throws his head back slightly.
Ross gestures emphatically, "I'm so sick of missing stuff. I want him for more than a day."
Ross adds, "I want him for a whole weekend." Carol responds, "No, listen, I mean, I feel like that"
Carol finishes, "would be great."
Ross's posture relaxes, and his expression softens.
Ross says, "Really? I mean, I had a whole speech prepared." Susan smiles and says, "Oh, shoot. That would have been fun."
Ross suddenly points down at the toddler in Carol's arms, his eyes widening.
Ross leans in closer to the child, saying urgently, "Did you just see that? Did you see? He just waved."
Ross continues pointing. "He just waved. He's never waved before. You've never waved before." Carol smiles and says gently, "Yes, he has. Very good."
The scene changes to a coffee shop. Rachel walks past a sofa carrying a mug. Monica sits reading on the sofa as Phoebe enters holding video tapes.
Rachel says, "Hey, Phoebe. What you got there?" Phoebe replies, "Okay."
Phoebe displays the tapes and says, "Love Story, Brian's Song, and Terms of Endearment."
Chandler sits on the sofa next to Monica.
Chandler says, "Wow. All you need now is The Killing Fields and some guacamole and you got yourself a party."
Phoebe takes off her jacket and says, "Yeah, I talked to my grandma about the"
Phoebe sits in a nearby chair and continues, "Old Yeller incident and she told me that my mom used to not show us the ends of sad movies"
Phoebe adds, "to shield us from the pain and"
Phoebe finishes, "sadness. You know, before she killed herself."
Phoebe looks down quietly.
Inside a coffee shop, Chandler and Joey walk forward. Joey begins to pull off his jacket.
Chandler says, "Hey,"
Monica sits on a sofa, looking up at them.
Monica asks, "where is he? Where's Richard?"
Chandler replies, "Did you ditch him? Yeah, right after we stole his lunch money and gave him a wedgie," while Joey takes off his jacket. Audience laughter sounds.
Monica gazes at them quietly.
Joey sits on the arm of the sofa and asks, "What's the matter with you? He's parking the car."
Joey continues, "Don't you guys have fun? Your boyfriend is so cool."
Monica asks, "Really?" Chandler responds, "Yeah, he let us drive his Jaguar. Joey for $12. Me for 15." Rachel says, "Wow,"
Rachel sits in an armchair nearby, leaning her chin on her hand.
Rachel says, "he must like you the best."
Joey stands up from the sofa's arm.
Joey says, "Oh, and what about that thing he did when he tipped the guy who showed us to our seats?"
Joey steps forward and adds, "He never even saw the money. It was like this. Hey,"
Joey shakes Chandler's hand, slipping him a folded bill, and says, "Chandler. Thanks for showing us to our seats."
Chandler looks down at the bill in his palm.
Chandler says, "You're welcome. Hey, Joey, thanks for parking the car," shaking Joey's hand and passing the bill back. Joey replies, "No problem."
Joey extends his hand again.
Joey shakes Chandler's hand, saying, "Hey, Chandler."
Chandler stops him, saying, "I think they get it."
Audience laughter is heard. Joey lowers his hands.
Richard enters the coffee shop. Chandler points to him, saying, "There's the man."
Joey says, "Hey," as he greets Richard.
Joey shakes hands with Richard.
Richard looks at the bill in his hand and says, "you're getting better. I'm going to keep this. By the way,"
Richard walks past them, holding up the bill. Audience laughter sounds.
Joey looks at Chandler and says, "he kept my dollar."
The setting changes to an exterior shot of a bridge over a river at dusk.
The setting changes to a night view of a city street. Now inside an apartment, Monica, Carol holding a bag, Susan, and Ross holding Ben stand in the living room.
Monica asks, "So, your first whole weekend without Ben. What are you guys going to do?" Carol begins, "We're going down to Colonial"
Carol finishes, "Williamsburg." Susan adds, "Yeah, a woman I went to college with just became the first female blacksmith down there."
Ross begins, "Well, you know, they're a little"
Ross finishes, "behind the times in Colonial Williamsburg."
Carol steps closer to Ben, pressing her face against his cheek.
Carol says to Ben, "Look, I better go before Mommy starts weeping. We love you."
Carol steps back.
Susan says, "Bye. Have a good time."
Carol and Susan turn toward the apartment door.
Carol opens the door and says, "Okay."
Carol says, "Bye. Ross?"
Ross replies, "Yeah. Look," as Carol and Susan exit.
Ross shuts the door and turns back. In the kitchen area, Joey flips a spatula in his hand while holding an unlit cigar in his mouth.
Monica and Ross watch Joey as he continues flipping the spatula.
Monica asks, "Joey, do you know we can see you from here?"
Joey pauses and gestures with the spatula.
Joey asks, "How come Richard looks so much cooler with one of these than me?"
Ross answers, "Well, for starters, you may want to light it and lose the spatula."
Monica walks toward the living room space.
Joey sets the spatula down. Monica says, "You know what? I think it's cute you're trying to be more like Richard."
Joey responds, "Not like him, per se. Just not unlike him."
Chandler enters the apartment, now sporting a mustache. He closes the door and stands still.
Monica smiles broadly as Chandler walks over to stand beside Joey. Ross stares at Chandler.
Ross says, "Look, it's the artist formerly known as Chandler."
Chandler says, "We're just trying something here, you know?" Monica says, "So,"
Monica asks, "Joey, why didn't you grow a mustache?" Joey replies, "Oh, we flipped for it. I got the cigar. He got the"
Chandler points to himself.
Chandler says, "figured if we both grew 'em, we'd look like dorks."
Ross continues holding his water bottle.
Ross says, "Yeah, you really sidestepped that landmine."
Joey says, "Hey, listen, we gotta go." Chandler adds, "I promised Richard we meet a oh, come on. You're"
Monica interrupts, "meeting Richard?" Joey replies, "Yeah, we're going to a Ranger game." Chandler asks, "Yeah, didn't he tell you?"
Chandler shakes his head slightly.
Monica says, "He told me he was going out with the guys."
Monica says, "I just didn't know that you were the guys." Chandler and Joey exchange a look and smile. Joey says, "You hear that? We're the guys. We're the guys"
Monica points her finger at Chandler.
Monica says, "with that mustache."
Monica asks, "Doesn't Chandler remind you of Aunt Sylvia?" Chandler replies, "Thank you."
Chandler raises his hand briefly. Joey places a hand on Chandler's back, and they both smile.
The view changes to an exterior shot of a city street corner, with superimposed images of Joey and Chandler briefly visible before fading.
In a busy coffee shop, Rachel wears an apron and stands near the counter. Ross approaches her, carrying a baby, Ben, in a front carrier.
"Hi. We're visiting," Ross says to Rachel, standing close to the counter.
Rachel smiles at Ross and Ben.
"It's Ben and his dad," Rachel says to Ben, smiling.
Rachel leans closer to Ben.
"Dada?" Rachel asks, opening her mouth wide.
Ross looks from Rachel to Ben.
"Can you say Dada?" Rachel asks Ben, raising her eyebrows.
Ross adjusts his hold on Ben.
"Look, I'm going to tell you mommies. You said it anyway, so you might as well try. Tough luck. No. A while ago I got a 'sa'" Ross says, looking down at Ben and then up at Rachel.
"out of him which I thought might turn into secondary caregiver. But" Ross continues, maintaining eye contact with Rachel.
Rachel smiles at Ross. Audience laughter is audible.
"hey, would you hold him for a SEC? Because I got to take this off. Oh, yeah, sure," Ross asks, pointing to his backpack. Rachel nods.
"Okay," Rachel says, reaching her hands forward.
Ross unclips Ben from his carrier and hands him to Rachel. She holds Ben with her arms fully extended away from her body.
Ross removes his backpack while Rachel continues to hold Ben at arm's length. Audience laughter is heard.
"What are you doing?" Ross asks, looking at Rachel's posture.
"I'm holding Ben," Rachel replies, keeping her arms rigid.
Ross stares at Rachel's extended arms.
"Yeah, well, he's a baby, not a bomb," Ross says, gesturing with his hand. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel looks down at Ben.
"Okay, well, just hold him like you'd hold a football," Ross says, gesturing as if holding a football.
Rachel grabs Ben by the waist and lets his legs dangle. "This is how I would hold a football," she says. "Okay," Ross responds, observing her grip.
"here. There we go. Okay. I'm sorry," Ross says, reaching over to position Ben against Rachel's chest. Rachel apologizes while Ross guides her arms.
"I'm just not very good with babies," Rachel says, looking down at Ben.
"I mean, I haven't been around them, I mean, since I was one," Rachel adds, looking back at Ross.
Ross touches Rachel's arm.
"All right. It's no big deal," Ross says, smiling slightly.
"Really?" Rachel asks. "Yeah, definitely. I'm sure you'll feel totally different when it's our baby," Ross replies, gesturing with his hand.
Rachel's smile fades and her gaze fixes on Ross.
"What," Rachel says, her mouth dropping open.
Ross looks at her.
"you think about stuff like that?" Rachel asks, her eyes wide. "Yeah," Ross answers, nodding.
Ross nods slightly.
"actually, I kind of think that we'll have two babies," Ross says, raising his eyebrows.
Rachel stares at Ross without moving.
"Two babies?" Rachel asks, her posture stiff. "Yeah, you know, a boy and a girl," Ross says, nodding.
"Hopefully, the girl will come first so Ben here won't feel too competitive," Ross continues, gesturing toward Ben with his thumb.
Rachel stares, her mouth slightly open. Audience laughter is audible.
"Then what's going to happen?" Rachel asks, her face expressionless.
"Well, we won't want to raise kids in the city, so we'll probably move to Scarsdale," Ross says, making sweeping hand gestures.
Rachel continues to stare.
"Yeah, that way I figure, you know, we'll be far enough away from our parents that we don't have to see them all the" Ross says, moving his hands.
"time but close enough that they can come over and babysit whenever we want. And yes, I know the taxes are a little" Ross continues, raising a finger.
"higher than, let's say, Nassau County. But the school system supposedly" Ross adds, gesturing with his hand.
Rachel's eyes widen as she interrupts, "wow."
Ross stops talking and looks at Rachel. Audience laughter is heard.
"Wow. That's great. Great," Rachel says, her gaze fixed on Ross.
Rachel swallows.
"Okay, wow," Rachel repeats, nodding slightly.
"You know what?" Rachel asks, shifting her weight.
"I'm off my break now," Rachel says, looking away briefly.
"So" Rachel adds, stepping forward.
Rachel moves to hand Ben back.
"here, you take this and" Rachel says, extending Ben toward Ross.
Ross quickly takes Ben from Rachel's arms.
"I am going to go pour these very nice people some coffee," Rachel says, gesturing toward the tables.
"Okay," Ross says, taking Ben fully. "Oh, Lord," Rachel says softly as she turns and walks quickly away.
Ross holds Ben and watches Rachel walk away. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross continues to stare in the direction Rachel left, his mouth slightly open.
